title: Task outcome: task-20260706-161251-Completion-Reviewer-10-data-
summary: Completion Reviewer 10 completed a read-only data architecture/performance review across dinobrain and dinobrain-data. Conclusion: AGREE with the intended index-first, SQLite-sharded, graph-aware, provenance-aware direction, but DISAGREE that the current system is scalability-complete. Current live data has operations.sqlite tracked/present, but no wiki.sqlite, no SQLite manifest, no dense-vectors index, and the persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy relative to the accepted corpus. Synthetic verifiers pass but report timings instead of enforcing latency budgets. Completion should require fresh wiki+operations SQLite shards, bounded indexed candidate selection, real BM25/dense retrieval constraints, graph adjacency freshness/health gates, cold-data archive/compaction policy, and explicit latency/freshness gates that fail when the prompt path regresses toward full-vault scans.
claim: Task outcome for DinoBrain: Completion Reviewer 10 completed a read-only data architecture/performance review across dinobrain and dinobrain-data. Conclusion: AGREE with the intended index-first, SQLite-sharded, graph-aware, provenance-aware direction, but DISAGREE that the current system is scalability-complete. Current live data has operations.sqlite tracked/present, but no wiki.sqlite, no SQLite manifest, no dense-vectors index, and the persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy relative to the accepted corpus. Synthetic verifiers pass but report timings instead of enforcing latency budgets. Completion should requi
decisions: AGREE with the direction only if SQLite wiki and operations shards are the required live fast path, not optional fallback artifacts.
DISAGREE that scalability completion is met while wiki.sqlite, manifest.json, and dense-vectors.json are absent from the live data index state.
Completion requires bounded candidate selection and latency budgets; current verifiers measure elapsed time but do not fail on agreed performance thresholds.
Completion requires graph health freshness against the current accepted corpus and graph adjacency/index metadata, not a stale healthy record.

Completion requires cold data compaction/archive boundaries so raw sessions, unreviewed candidates, old tasks/traces/context packs, and broad accepted behavior rules do not dominate default retrieval.
next_steps: Create or refresh live .dino/index/sqlite/wiki.sqlite and .dino/index/sqlite/manifest.json, then verify get_context_pack and wiki_search report candidate_source=sqlite_shards_v2 on the real data repo.
Add hard latency/freshness gates for prompt preflight, wiki_search, context pack retrieval, recent task lookup, index rebuild, and compaction/archive jobs at synthetic 10k/50k+ memory sizes.
Replace leading-wildcard term expansion with SQLite FTS5/BM25 or another bounded sparse retrieval path, and make dense retrieval top-K rather than loading/considering every vector from JSON.
Make graph-health fail when stale relative to wiki index/shard generation time or when accepted corpus/source lineage is missing; include adjacency/node/edge counts from the current index.

Define hot/warm/cold retention: raw sessions stay local-only and excluded, operational logs are partitioned/archived by time/project, and accepted behavior memories are type-budgeted so they do not swamp durable Wiki/Source/Project records.

Conclusion: AGREE with the intended index-first, SQLite-sharded, graph-aware, provenance-aware direction, but DISAGREE that the current system is scalability-complete. Current live data has operations.sqlite tracked/present, but no wiki.sqlite, no SQLite manifest, no dense-vectors index, and the persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy relative to the accepted corpus. Synthetic verifiers pass but report timings instead of enforcing latency budgets. Completion should require fresh wiki+operations SQLite shards, bounded indexed candidate selection, real BM25/dense retrieval constraints, graph adjacency freshness/health gates, cold-data archive/compaction policy, and explicit latency/freshness gates that fail when the prompt path regresses toward full-vault scans.
